feedback.mixpanel-compact-analysis: I like the filters, which are very compact in one line, and they also have chips activated when the filter is selected. Also the graph is very compact and small and has small numbers and a y-axis. It also has a small one-line explanation of what each color is and then there's a table below that's very simple, compact, thin, that fits on one screen and doesn't need to scroll to the sides.
feedback.amplitude-segmentation: I like the graph, mostly the fact that the filter are in the top right.
feedback.better-stack-reporting: This is the opposite of what I'm looking even though it's use case is similar. The dense incident table may be attractive but the status metrics occupy too much space and could be better analyzed as part of the graph.
feedback.kajabi-pageviews: It should hide filter complexity away from the main report surface. Agree with that. But the circles in the bottom occupy too much space without providing extra information
feedback.fibery-report-builder: This is too much flexibility. I want a fixed dashboard. Don't need to add or subtract any graph or to change the configuration and view besides filtering and grouping data.
feedback.zendesk-ticket-report: I don't understand this example or this reference. That's why I'm not picking it up and including it in the other tab.

mixpanel-compact-analysis::mixpanel-comp.note: I like these flat filters that don't occupy a lot of space.
We don't need something like this.
And we don't need a menu. It occupies too much space.
This table is a perfect width. The one we have for the alerts is too wide and we need to scroll horizontally and that's terrible. Also the size of each row is very comfortable and compact.

amplitude-segmentation::amplitude-segmen.note: Even though having something like this is cool, I don't think we need it. Our information is not that configurable.
These filters look good and don't occupy a lot of space. Clicking on chips is much nicer than a drop-down select option.
This export CSV button is much smaller and placed in a less important place.
The header is not that big as the one we have in our design and it doesn't occupy a lot of space saying stuff that we don't need.
